rationale: Ground truth is thin (two LGTM approvals plus a validation-result request that was satisfied, and a naming/packed_modules_mapping suggestion likely in an unshown diff hunk); neither candidate recalls that specific mapper suggestion, but both independently converge on the analogous 'default_components duplicates other declarative sources of truth' observation, which is well grounded via repo greps. X additionally notes the PR's before/after validation images were provided, directly mirroring the real xuechendi/tthakkal exchange, and surfaces a genuinely valuable extra gap (text-encoder LoRA silently no-op) that Y misses. Y is slightly more systematic with explicit validated/sweep evidence trails and a couple of solid nits (missing SDXL integration test, undocumented _lora_components), but doesn't touch the validation topic and its findings largely overlap X's core point. Both are well grounded with no clear fabrications; neither includes a LATENT GAP CHECK section.
